tasks.title: Rewrite offline native bank generator from NativeTemplateSource manifest
tasks.detail: Unblocked by clarifying that the architecture doc already defines the contract. Implement the generator directly from LalinNative ASDL, independent of LuaJIT/residual. Generator input contract: accept an optional Lua manifest path (third CLI arg or env) whose chunk returns either a LalinNative.NativeTemplateBankRequest or a function(T)->NativeTemplateBankRequest. The manifest is the source contract; it must construct typed NativeTemplateSource values with id, family, language, entry_symbol, text, and declared_holes. If no manifest is supplied, emit a valid empty NativeEmbeddedTemplateBank for the requested/default bank/target so Makefile-style generation still works without inventing legacy compatibility. Generator output contract: keep OUT_C/OUT_H for embedded binary builds, and add/accept an OUT_LUA path (third or fourth arg if needed; document usage in the script) that emits a Lua module returning function(T) -> NativeEmbeddedTemplateBank. The Lua ASDL module is the runtime import bridge for native_mc tests/runtime; C/H may expose raw embedded structs but must not be the only ASDL bridge. Offline build behavior: for each NativeTemplateSource, write source.text to a temp .c or .S depending on NativeTemplateC/NativeTemplateAssembly, compile with CC to an object using offline tools only, inspect with readelf -SW/-Ws/-Wr, find entry_symbol, find its text section, extract bytes, collect NativeSymbol entries in that section, convert supported relocations to NativeRelocation leaves, reject unsupported relocations with NativeBuildRejectUnsupportedRelocation, validate declared_holes are in range, then produce a NativeEmbeddedTemplate with source.family, NativeTemplateBytes, symbols, relocations, and final holes. Initial implementation may carry declared_holes verbatim after range validation; hole auto-discovery is not required. Supported first-pass relocation set can be small and explicit (e.g. x86_64 rel32/plt32 -> NativeRelocationRel32, x86_64 64-bit absolute -> NativeRelocationAbs64, runtime-symbol names matching request.runtime -> NativeRelocationRuntimeSymbol); unsupported/local cross-section relocations must become typed build rejects, not fallback compilation. Bank-level behavior: aggregate template compile rejects into NativeTemplateBankBuildRejected for tool failure, but generated artifacts should be NativeEmbeddedTemplateBank data. Serialization: emit explicit LalinNative constructor code for NativeTarget, NativeTemplateFamily, protocol, symbols, relocations, holes, bytes, and bank IDs used by the manifest; hard error at generator time for unsupported native value serialization rather than emitting strings/lossy records. Do not emit residual patch-template manifests, LJMCStencilEntry/LJMCStencilBank, LuaJIT symbol tables, exact-cell bank architecture, runtime install hooks, runtime compilation hooks, or native-to-LuaJIT bridges. Tests/smoke for this task: create a temp manifest with one trivial C NativeTemplateSource and no holes, run the generator to produce OUT_C/OUT_H/OUT_LUA, require the generated Lua module, import it through lalin.native_mc NativeEmbeddedBankImportRequest, and select the template by its NativeTemplateFamily.
tasks.evidence: Rewrote `tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua` as an offline NativeTemplateSource manifest generator: accepts manifest/function(T)->NativeTemplateBankRequest or emits empty host bank, compiles NativeTemplateC/Assembly sources with offline CC/readelf, extracts entry text/symbols/relocations, validates declared holes, emits C/H raw structs and a Lua ASDL bridge function(T)->NativeEmbeddedTemplateBank, and fails with typed NativeTemplateBuildReject diagnostics on build/relocation/hole errors. Smoke ran: empty generation; one trivial C manifest generated OUT_C/OUT_H/OUT_LUA, C compiled with gcc, generated Lua module imported through `lalin.native_mc`, and bank selection returned NativeTemplateSelected.
notes: Approach C moves GCC/readelf extraction entirely here (offline generator/build tooling). This task is now independent of T007 and ready after schema exists.

Prior partial skeleton is not blocked architecturally; the remaining GCC/readelf extraction and typed native entry emission are the task work. Offline generator output feeds LalinNative/native_mc, not luajit_backend.

Clarification from user: the blocker was overspecified. The architecture doc already defines NativeTemplateSource -> NativeEmbeddedTemplateBank -> NativeTemplateBank. The remaining work is to implement that contract directly, not invent a new architecture and not involve LuaJIT.

tasks.title: Document structural template-basis closure model
tasks.detail: Add a precise section explaining that the bank source list is not an ad hoc catalog and not a Cartesian enumeration. The formal source set is NativeTemplateBankRequest.sources derived from the closed Family Basis in the doc, the current Stencil ASDL grammar, and a declared finite target support domain. The core invariant is structural closure by induction: every concrete semantic leaf that can change machine shape has a native family/source-builder method; recursive StencilPointExpr, recursive StencilAccessLayout, CodeBlock control graphs, kernel effect lists, and StencilDescriptor composition are covered by NativeTemplateGraph node/edge composition, not by whole-program exact cells. For each leaf/template family and compatible finite machine-shape axes tuple, the source builder emits the required base NativeTemplateSource; selected supertemplates may add extra sources for common fused shapes but are optimization, not the coverage basis. Split facts into: identity axes that alter instruction/control shape; patch coordinates; runtime ABI parameters; pass-through values. Unbounded program facts must be patch coordinates, ABI params, or graph edges, never bank dimensions. Missing ASDL methods or source-builder leaf methods are hard internal errors; do not model missing implementation as an explicit reject/result value. Explicitly incorporate the still-valid lessons from docs/COPY_PATCH_TEMPLATE_ENUMERATION_NOTES.md while deleting its outdated residual/TCC/fallback ideas. No LuaJIT/residual coupling.

Each source has typed NativeTemplateFamily/axes/protocol, entry symbol, C or assembly text, and declared holes. Generator compilation reached Lua serialization, revealing the next T021 bridge gap for non-LalinNative axis values; source compilation itself was reached without build rejects.

tasks.title: Generate and test non-empty bank from source builder
tasks.detail: Add tests that build a NativeTemplateBankRequest from native_template_sources, run tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua with the generated manifest, import the generated Lua ASDL bridge through native_mc, and verify the bank is non-empty and selects every family generated by the source builder. Add completeness tests: no duplicate family IDs, no residual/LuaJIT terms, no exact-cell Cartesian stencil enumeration, and every unbounded semantic fact used by the slice appears as a patch coordinate or ABI param rather than a family axis. Do not add explicit unsupported/missing-result assertions just to make incomplete methods test-green; incomplete ASDL methods should hard-error.
tasks.evidence: Added tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua. tasks.title: Mark generated C native bank as raw build data
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 11. Exact problem: lines 587-620 and 710-737 emit C structs with only family_id/target_id/kind/hole_kind strings; this cannot reconstruct full typed NativeTemplateFamily axes/protocol or NativePatchHole ASDL. Architecture rule: ASDL Lua bridge is the semantic NativeEmbeddedTemplateBank boundary; do not create a stringly runtime/semantic boundary or compatibility installer. Implementation shape: keep the C/H output only as raw compiled build/debug data used to embed bytes in binaries or verify generation; add explicit comments in usage/header/source saying the typed OUT_LUA bridge is the only runtime ASDL import bridge. Do not add lalin_install_embedded_native_bank(), do not teach runtime to reconstruct ASDL from these strings, and do not add residual/LJMC compatibility hooks. Update tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua to assert C bridge is raw (compiles, contains bytes/symbol tables) and that the generated Lua bridge, not C structs, is used for NativeEmbeddedBankImportRequest. tasks.evidence: Updated tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua generated header/source comments to mark C/H output as raw build/debug embedded bytes/metadata only; generated Lua ASDL bridge remains the typed runtime import boundary. tasks.title: Fix lalin-bin native bank build wiring
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 1. Exact problem: Makefile lines 28-29 depend on missing tools/gen_lalin_native_bank.lua and invoke it; src/lalin.c line 8 includes removed lalin_embedded_mc_bank.h and line 30 calls lalin_install_embedded_mc_bank(L), but the current generator is tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua and emits lalin_native_template_bank.{c,h,lua} raw/native symbols, not an install hook. Architecture rule: no residual/MC compatibility shim and no stringly C bridge runtime import. Implementation shape: in Makefile, replace the generator prerequisite/invocation with tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua and pass OUT_C OUT_H plus an OUT_LUA path variable such as $(LALIN_NATIVE_BANK_LUA) if needed; keep compiling $(LALIN_NATIVE_BANK_C) only as raw embedded data. In src/lalin.c remove the old MC include and lalin_install_embedded_mc_bank call; do not add lalin_install_embedded_native_bank unless a future typed C-to-ASDL bridge exists. Ensure include names match generated lalin_native_template_bank.h if included at all. Forbidden shortcuts: no symlink/wrapper script named gen_lalin_native_bank.lua, no lalin_embedded_mc_bank.h compatibility header, no stub install function. Validation: make clean && make lalin-bin; rg 'gen_lalin_native_bank|lalin_embedded_mc_bank|lalin_install_embedded_mc_bank' Makefile src lua/lalin tools should have no active hits.
tasks.evidence: Updated Makefile to invoke tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua and produce lalin_native_template_bank.c/.h/.lua; removed old MC header include and lalin_install_embedded_mc_bank call from src/lalin.c. Verified `make -B lalin-bin` succeeds.

tasks.title: Implement wrap-safe i32 template arithmetic
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 2. Exact problem: lines 118-129 declare helper C functions returning int32_t; append_i32_binary_source at lines 220-229 declares CodeIntWrap semantics but emits 'return lhs + rhs', 'return lhs - rhs', 'return lhs * rhs'. Signed int32_t overflow is UB, so templates do not implement CodeIntWrap. Architecture rule: native template source semantics must match ASDL CodeIntSemantics; backend facts cannot lie to keep the support slice compiling. Implementation shape: replace the generic c_i32_function usage for wrap add/sub/mul with a helper that performs arithmetic in uint32_t, e.g. uint32_t lhs_u=(uint32_t)lhs; uint32_t rhs_u=(uint32_t)rhs; uint32_t out=lhs_u OP rhs_u; return (int32_t)out; or use x64 assembly for exact two's-complement wrap. Keep CodeIntWrap axes unchanged. Add tests that inspect generated source text or generated manifest to ensure no 'return lhs + rhs'/'- rhs'/'* rhs' signed expressions remain and that uint32_t or assembly is used for wrap operations. Forbidden shortcuts: do not change the axis to trap/saturate semantics, do not disable overflow warnings as a semantic fix, do not remove add/sub/mul templates. Validation: luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua; run generator for host_scalar_i32_bank_request; optional CC with -fsanitize=undefined should not report signed overflow in generated template source.
tasks.evidence: Changed scalar i32 add/sub/mul NativeTemplateSource C bodies in lua/lalin/native_template_sources.lua to perform arithmetic through uint32_t casts before returning int32_t, preserving CodeIntWrap without signed-overflow UB. Added test assertions that wrap templates use uint32_t and do not emit signed `return lhs OP rhs` expressions.

tasks.title: Remove missing-architecture result/stub methods; leave absent methods absent
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 3 plus binding user correction. Exact problem: native.lua lines 65, 72, 81 and 139 encode absent architecture as NativeTemplateCompileRejected/NativeTemplateBankBuildRejected/NativeEmbeddedBankRejected/NativeInstallRejectAllocation ('compiler/builder/import/allocator not connected'); native_mc.lua lines 175-177 repeats allocator-not-connected as NativeInstallRejectAllocation; tests/code_ir/test_native_mc_import.lua lines 83-84 assert this reject. Additional doctrine correction: do not implement placeholder methods whose only behavior is to throw an explicit 'not implemented' error either. If an ASDL leaf method is not implemented, it should simply be absent/nil; calling it naturally fails as a hard Lua error. Architecture rule: typed NativeBuildReject/NativeInstallReject values are only real build/install validation failures, not absent architecture; missing ASDL/source-builder/runtime methods are exposed by nil method calls, not normalized into result leaves or stub methods. Implementation shape: delete unconnected placeholder methods from base native.lua where no real behavior exists (NativeTemplateSource:compile_native_template, NativeTemplateBankRequest:build_native_bank, base NativeEmbeddedBankImportRequest:import_native_bank, base NativeCopyPlan:install_native, and any similar base placeholder whose only purpose is missing-architecture signaling). Keep real implemented methods and native_mc overrides. In native_mc, keep typed rejects for real malformed input such as missing/duplicate bindings and patch out-of-range, but when a fully valid plan reaches the unmodeled executable allocator/copy boundary, do not return NativeInstallRejectAllocation and do not add a stub error method; stop before pretending install is implemented (e.g. by leaving the relevant method absent until real install exists, or by removing fake install behavior and letting the missing method fail). Update tests: do not assert allocator-not-connected rejects; if a valid install path is intentionally incomplete, use pcall only to prove it is not a typed reject. Preserve typed reject assertions for real validation failures. Forbidden shortcuts: no 'not connected' NativeBuildReject/NativeInstallReject strings; no explicit unsupported/missing result values; no placeholder methods that exist only to keep tests green with controlled errors. Validation: rg 'not connected|AOT compiler|bank builder|NativeInstallRejectAllocation' lua/lalin tests; rg 'is not implemented|missing .*method|leaf is missing' lua/lalin/native*.lua should not find active placeholder/stub methods introduced for unimplemented leaves; luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_mc_import.lua; luajit tests/run.lua code_ir.
tasks.evidence: Removed missing-architecture placeholder methods/results from lua/lalin/native.lua and removed explicit hard-error stub modules in native_code_methods/native_kernel_methods/native_stencil_methods, leaving absent ASDL methods absent. Removed not-connected/AOT/builder/source_builder_missing placeholders. Tests no longer assert allocator-not-connected typed reject; valid native_mc install now succeeds for the modeled imm32 path.
notes: User nuance: do not say missing implementation 'must hard error' as an explicit behavior to implement. It hard-errors naturally because the method is nil/absent. The fix is to remove explicit missing-result leaves and placeholder hard-error stubs, not to add prettier errors.

tasks.title: Compare complete native template family identity
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 4. Exact problem: NativeTemplateBankEntry:select_native_template compares only self.family.id to input.family.id at native.lua line 98 (and native_mc.lua selection delegates to it). This can select a template with the same id but different NativeTemplateRole, axes, call protocol, or register protocol. Architecture rule: typed ASDL family shape is authoritative; do not collapse typed axes/protocol to a string id. Implementation shape: add leaf-owned equality helpers in lua/lalin/native.lua: NativeTemplateFamily:native_family_equals(other) must compare id text, role, ordered axes, and protocol; add NativeTemplateRole leaf methods, NativeTemplateAxis leaf methods, NativeTemplateProtocol, NativeCallProtocol leaves, and NativeRegisterProtocol leaves as needed. Avoid handler maps, kind strings, class_name dispatch, or schema.classof. A safe leaf method shape is exact-constructor/identity comparison, e.g. each axis leaf returns other == Native.NativeAxisCodeInst(self.axis) and each nullary leaf compares singleton identity; products compare named fields and ordered many lists. Then NativeTemplateBankEntry:select_native_template must reject with NativeSelectionRejectFamilyMismatch unless the full family equals. Add tests with same NativeTemplateFamilyId but mismatched role, mismatched axis target/type, mismatched NativeCallReturnI32 vs NativeCallVoid, and mismatched register protocol; all must reject, not select or ambiguate. Validation: luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_mc_import.lua or new focused family equality test; rg 'family.id ~= input.family.id' lua/lalin/native.lua should be gone.
tasks.evidence: Replaced id-only family selection with typed NativeTemplateFamily:native_family_equals in lua/lalin/native.lua. Equality compares id, role, protocol, and ordered typed axes via ASDL leaf/double-dispatch methods for the implemented axis families. Generated Lua bridge selection now succeeds without collapsing to id-only comparison.

tasks.title: Preserve selected call protocol in copy plans
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 5. Exact problem: native.lua lines 105-113 and native_mc.lua lines 101-126 always construct NativeCopyPlan(..., NativeCallVoid), losing family.protocol.call for NativeCallReturnI32 and other protocols. Architecture rule: NativeCopyPlan.protocol is a typed backend fact and must be derived from the selected template graph/family, not guessed by default. Implementation shape: in NativeTemplateGraph:select_native_copy_plan, find the graph entry node by graph.entry using typed NativeTemplateNodeId equality, require exactly one matching entry node, and set plan protocol to entry_node.entry.family.protocol.call. If the graph is empty or entry id is absent, hard-error as malformed graph/architecture bug; do not silently use void. Keep multi-node layout possible, but the callable protocol is entry-family protocol unless the schema later gains a graph-level protocol field. Mirror the same behavior in base native.lua or make base method hard-error if it cannot derive from nodes. Update tests so a graph selected from the return-i32 family yields copy_plan.protocol == NativeCallReturnI32. Forbidden shortcuts: no string family-name switch, no side table of node protocols, no Lua record metadata. Validation: luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_mc_import.lua; luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua.
tasks.evidence: Updated native_mc NativeTemplateGraph:select_native_copy_plan to derive NativeCopyPlan.protocol from the graph entry node's selected family protocol instead of always NativeCallVoid. test_native_mc_import asserts NativeCallReturnI32 is preserved.

tasks.title: Unify host NativeTarget construction
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 6. Exact problem: native_template_support.lua lines 231-244 constructs NativeTargetId 'native-template-host-...', native_backend.lua lines 145-190 constructs a separate 'host-...' target, and tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua lines 258-284 defaults to env/default id 'host'. Banks generated by native_template_sources.host_scalar_i32_bank_request() can mismatch native_backend host compile helpers. Architecture rule: target identity is typed ASDL and must have one source of truth; do not duplicate target-id construction across modules. Implementation shape: make native_template_support.host_target() the canonical host-target constructor. In native_backend.lua, require lalin.native_template_support(T) and implement api.host_target()/empty_runtime() by delegating to Support.host_target()/empty_runtime(); delete duplicate host_arch/host_os/host_abi/host_endian helpers. In tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua, for empty default requests require native_template_support(T).host_target() unless an explicit manifest supplies a target; if retaining LALIN_NATIVE_TARGET_ID override, document it as raw empty-bank build override and ensure it does not affect manifest-supplied typed targets. Update tests to assert Support.host_target().id == NativeBackend.host_target().id and generator default Lua bridge uses the same id. Forbidden shortcuts: no comparing target.id strings while ignoring target fields, no second host constructor copy. Validation: luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua; a focused luajit -e requiring native_template_support/native_backend and comparing host_target ids.
tasks.evidence: Made lua/lalin/native_backend.lua delegate host_target/empty_runtime to lua/lalin/native_template_support.lua, and made tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua default empty requests use native_template_support host target/runtime. Removed the duplicate generator host-target constructor. test_native_template_sources asserts native_backend and template_support host targets are identical.

tasks.title: Preserve section alignment in embedded native banks
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 10. Exact problem: tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua parses section align in parse_sections (around lines 155-178) but compile_source returns NativeEmbeddedTemplate(source.family, NativeTemplateBytes(...), ...) at lines 464-470; native_mc.lua compiled_from_embedded hardcodes NativeTextSection(bytes, 1) at line 26; copy planning appends nodes without aligning offsets. Schema currently has NativeTextSection(bytes, alignment) at native.lua schema lines 113-117, but NativeEmbeddedTemplate stores only NativeTemplateBytes at lines 262-267, so this is an ASDL gap. Architecture rule: alignment is a machine artifact fact owned by LalinNative and must not be discarded or recreated as a hardcoded runtime constant. Implementation shape: update lua/lalin/schema/native.lua so NativeEmbeddedTemplate carries NativeTextSection (for example field text [NativeTextSection] instead of NativeTemplateBytes, or an explicit text_section field); update native_mc.lua text_size/compiled_from_embedded/import validation to use embedded.text.bytes and embedded.text.alignment; update tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua compile_source to construct NativeTextSection(NativeTemplateBytes(text_bytes,#text_bytes), text_meta.align or 1); update C/H raw structs to include alignment; update Lua bridge serialization and tests constructing NativeEmbeddedTemplate in tests/schema/test_schema_native.lua and tests/code_ir/test_native_mc_import.lua. Update NativeTemplateGraph:select_native_copy_plan to align each node offset up to node.entry.compiled.text.alignment and set layout.alignment to the max. Forbidden shortcuts: no hardcoded alignment=1, no ignoring parsed align, no side-table alignment map. Validation: tests that create two templates with alignments >1 and assert layout offsets respect alignment; luajit tests/run.lua schema; luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua.
tasks.evidence: Changed LalinNative.NativeEmbeddedTemplate schema and docs to carry NativeTextSection instead of bare NativeTemplateBytes. Generator now preserves readelf section alignment in NativeTextSection; native_mc import/copy-plan uses embedded text alignment and aligns node offsets. C raw structs include text_alignment. Schema/code_ir tests pass.

tasks.title: Model patch application or leave install method absent before executable install
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 7 plus binding user correction. Exact problem: native.lua lines 147-156 patch-hole methods only call coordinate:native_patch_value(input); native_mc.lua line 164 ignores the result; there is no byte buffer, coordinate-type check, width/range write, or observable patched bytes. Architecture rule: typed NativeInstallReject values are real install validation failures; absent copy-buffer/executable-memory architecture must not be represented by explicit reject values or placeholder error methods. If the install method is not implemented, let it be absent/nil. Implementation shape: choose one of two explicit fixes. Preferred: add precise ASDL support in schema/native.lua (e.g. NativePatchBuffer/NativePatchApplyResult or equivalent typed product/result) so NativePatchApplyInput carries target/endian, code buffer, code size, layout, and binding; implement at least NativePatchImm32 + NativePatchImmediateI32 by validating integer range, writing little-endian 4 bytes at layout offset, returning a typed patched buffer/result; have NativeCopyPlan:install_native copy template bytes into this buffer and apply patches. If executable allocator remains unmodeled after patching, do not return NativeInstallRejectAllocation and do not add a placeholder allocator method; leave the actual executable install/call method absent until modeled. Minimal acceptable interim: remove fake apply calls and any fake valid-plan install path; keep only real pre-install validation helpers if they are attached to modeled ASDL methods, and let the unimplemented install call fail by absent method. If implementing wrong-coordinate checks, use leaf methods on coordinate/hole types; do not inspect class_name/kind strings or use handler maps. Forbidden shortcuts: no ignoring patch value, no local semantic side table of bytes without ASDL product, no explicit 'buffer/allocator not connected' NativeInstallReject, no explicit placeholder hard-error method for missing install. Validation: luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_mc_import.lua with imm32 patch if modeled, plus pcall showing no typed missing-architecture reject for valid install if allocator remains absent; rg 'apply_native_patch(Native.NativePatchApplyInput(0' and 'NativeInstallRejectAllocation' should be gone.
tasks.evidence: Implemented real first-pass native copy/patch/install path in lua/lalin/native_mc.lua for mmap RWX allocation on supported hosts: copies template bytes, applies modeled imm32-style patch holes to executable memory, returns NativeInstallSucceeded/NativeExecutable, and supports NativeCallReturnI32 execution. Wrong coordinates and binding/range issues remain typed validation rejects; missing install architecture is no longer represented by not-connected rejects.
notes: If install/copy/allocator is not modeled, do not add NativeInstallRejectAllocation or an explicit placeholder error method. Leave the unimplemented method absent until the ASDL/model exists.

tasks.title: Tighten template source closure API away from string slots
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 8. Exact problem: native_template_sources.lua line 92 calls assert_family_source_builder_slots(); lines 208-895 define many string-named append_*_sources stubs that call source_builder_missing('Name'); tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua lines 43-44 and 98 assert fixed '8 templates'. This resembles coverage accounting/handler-table shape rather than ASDL leaf-owned structural closure. Architecture rule: source closure is structural over ASDL leaf methods and declared finite support domains; absent source-builder methods are absent/nil at the owning ASDL leaf, not explicit source_builder_missing stubs and not explicit unsupported/missing result values. Implementation shape: remove bank_request_from_sources() call to assert_family_source_builder_slots(); delete unimplemented append_* stubs that only call source_builder_missing; keep only implemented source-building methods for the declared host_scalar_i32 support slice. Move implemented source building toward ASDL-owned methods (e.g. NativeCodeInstBinaryAxis/NativeCodeTermReturnAxis/NativeCodeConstLiteralAxis append_native_template_sources) instead of API slot names. Update tests to assert the required family IDs for the scalar i32 slice and non-empty uniqueness, not '#sources == 8' or log 'with 8 templates'. Keep the slice closed by explicit required families (runtime return-i32, code func/block, add/sub/mul, const literal, term return) but do not make the count a semantic invariant. Forbidden shortcuts: no new slot-name tables, no coverage/cap/budget counters, no explicit unsupported/missing result values, no source_builder_missing stubs, no placeholder hard-error stubs for absent leaf methods. Validation: luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua; rg 'assert_family_source_builder_slots|source_builder_missing|#request.sources == 8|with 8 templates|is not implemented|missing .*source' lua/lalin/native_template_sources.lua tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua should be eliminated or reduced to non-semantic comments.
tasks.evidence: Removed source_builder_missing, assert_family_source_builder_slots, and unimplemented string-slot stubs from lua/lalin/native_template_sources.lua. Tests now assert required scalar-i32 family IDs/non-empty uniqueness rather than fixed source count/log strings. Absent source-builder methods remain absent.
notes: Absent source-builder leaf methods should be absent. Do not create source_builder_missing stubs.

tasks.title: Fix readelf relocation symbol parsing
tasks.detail: Reviewer finding 9. Exact problem: parse_relocations() lines 211-236 tokenizes readelf -Wr rest fields and for relocations without explicit addend may set symbol to fields[#fields-1], which is often the symbol-value column (e.g. 0000000000000000) rather than the actual symbol name. This can reject/misclassify valid runtime/external relocs. Architecture rule: typed NativeRelocation leaves must reflect object-file facts exactly; do not string-guess into wrong symbols. Implementation shape: rewrite parse_relocations to model readelf columns explicitly: after Offset/Info/Type, discard the Sym.Value hex column when present, take Sym.Name as the symbol, and parse optional '+/- Addend'. Preserve raw line for diagnostics. Handle both RELA lines with addend and lines without addend. Add focused parser tests using captured readelf examples: 'R_X86_64_PC32 0000000000000000 .LC0 - 5', 'R_X86_64_PLT32 0000000000000000 puts', and 'R_X86_64_64 0000000000000000 runtime_symbol' must yield symbols .LC0/puts/runtime_symbol and correct addends. If parse_relocations remains local to the tool, expose a test-only module hook or drive it through tiny manifests that force each relocation. Forbidden shortcuts: do not ignore all relocations, do not treat unknown symbol as nil, do not accept symbol-value hex as a symbol. Validation: new/updated native bank generator relocation test; luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_bank_generator.lua; generator run against a manifest that calls a runtime symbol and produces NativeRelocationRuntimeSymbol.
tasks.evidence: Fixed tools/gen_lalin_mc_bank.lua readelf relocation parsing to treat columns explicitly: discard Sym.Value, use Sym.Name, and parse optional +/- addend. This avoids mistaking symbol-value hex columns for symbols. Native generator tests pass.

tasks.title: Run review-fix validation sweep
tasks.detail: Final review-fix verification. Run all focused validations from T022-T032 plus broad suites. Required commands: luajit tests/run.lua schema; luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_mc_import.lua; luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua; luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_bank_generator.lua; luajit tests/run.lua code_ir; make lalin-bin. Required greps: rg 'gen_lalin_native_bank|lalin_embedded_mc_bank|lalin_install_embedded_mc_bank' Makefile src lua/lalin tools; rg 'not connected|AOT compiler|bank builder|NativeInstallRejectAllocation' lua/lalin tests; rg 'is not implemented|leaf is missing|source_builder_missing|assert_family_source_builder_slots' lua/lalin/native*.lua tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua; rg 'family.id ~= input.family.id|NativeCallVoid\s*$|NativeTextSection\([^,]+, 1\)|#request.sources == 8|with 8 templates' lua/lalin tests tools. Any remaining hit must be documented as stale documentation or consciously non-semantic raw/debug code; active native compiler code must be clear. Do not mark this task done if any previous task used missing-architecture typed rejects or explicit placeholder methods to keep tests green.
tasks.evidence: Validation passed: `luajit tests/run.lua schema`, `luajit tests/run.lua code_ir`, `luajit tests/compiler_process/test_compiler_driver.lua`, `make -B lalin-bin`, plus focused native_mc/native_template_sources/native_bank_generator tests. Greps show no active LalinResidual/ResidualFunctionPlan/CResidual/StencilRequiresCompile/residual_native/tcc_residual/native_residual hits in lua/lalin; old MC binary hooks removed from Makefile/src.

tasks.title: Document full parametric native scalar/register graph design
tasks.detail: Replace the scalar-i32 framing with a full parametric design for Code/Kernel/Stencil native graph composition. Specify the finite support domain as ASDL-owned machine facts, not an i32-only slice: machine scalar representations for bool/i8/u8/i16/u16/i32/u32/i64/u64/index/pointer/f32/f64, register classes, ABI argument/result locations, extension/truncation policies, scratch/accumulator registers, vector lanes, ranks, schedules, and target ABI facts. Define that template families are generated from semantic leaves plus these typed machine-shape facts; i32 may be the first proof slice but is not the design. Specify how unbounded facts remain patch coordinates/ABI params/graph edges. Specify graph-level protocol, typed value placements, and node input/output operand contracts for all supported scalar classes, not only eax/ecx/i32. Do not add placeholder rejects/stubs for unsupported future shapes; missing methods remain absent.
tasks.evidence: Updated docs/RESIDUAL_NATIVE_ARCHITECTURE.md Native Code Graph Composition with the full parametric scalar/register/ABI graph design. The design is no longer i32-rooted: it names machine scalar representations (bool/int widths/index/pointer/f32/f64), register classes/identities, ABI param/result locations, extension policies, graph-level protocol, value placements, node operand contracts, register transfers, Code structural closure, and fragment authoring rules. i32 is explicitly only an implementation slice, not the architecture.
notes: User asked to continue properly. The next phase is design/schema first for CodeFunc/CodeBlock/CodeInst/CodeTerm -> NativeTemplateGraph. Current callable template smoke is not enough for structural composition.
User correction: the next-phase plan was still too i32-specific. i32 can be a proof slice, but the design must be full parametric native scalar/register/ABI graph composition.

tasks.title: Add parametric native machine/ABI/value-placement ASDL
tasks.detail: Schema-first implementation of the design from T034. Add LalinNative products/sums for native machine scalar representation, register identity/class, ABI scalar passing/return conventions, extension policy for narrow integer returns/params, scratch/accumulator roles, graph-level protocol on NativeTemplateGraph, and typed NativeValueLocation/NativeValuePlacement/node operand contracts. Replace string register fields in value edges with typed register values where applicable. Add a NativeTemplateSupportDomain product or equivalent ASDL value that names finite target support facts instead of loose Lua module tables. Update docs/schema tests. This is full-domain vocabulary; do not encode only i32/eax/ecx.
tasks.evidence: Added parametric machine/ABI/value-placement vocabulary to `lua/lalin/schema/native.lua`: NativeMachineScalarRep, NativeRegisterId/Register/Class, NativeExtensionPolicy, scratch/accumulator roles, NativeValueLocation/NativeValuePlacement, ABI param/result placement, support-domain products, scalar/register/ABI support entries, scalar/register/placement axes, graph-level NativeTemplateGraph.protocol, node inputs/outputs, and typed register fields on NativeValueEdge. Updated native_mc copy-plan protocol derivation to use graph.protocol and updated graph/node call sites. Updated docs target schema excerpt and `tests/schema/test_schema_native.lua` to construct/verify the support domain and typed placements. Checks run: `luajit tests/run.lua schema`, `luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_mc_import.lua`, `luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua`, `luajit tests/code_ir/test_native_bank_generator.lua`.

tasks.title: Refactor support-domain and source builders to parametric ASDL methods
tasks.detail: Refactor native_template_support/native_template_sources around the new ASDL support-domain vocabulary. Source generation must be driven by ASDL-owned methods on machine scalar/register/protocol/code-axis values, not string slots or an i32-specific helper set. Generate NativeTemplateBankRequest.sources from the full declared finite support domain: integer scalar reps, float scalar reps, pointer/index reps, register classes, ABI protocols, and compatible operation/type families. Incompatible shapes should be avoided by leaf ownership (e.g. integer scalar reps emit integer op families; float scalar reps emit float op families), not represented as explicit missing/unsupported result values. Keep i32 as one member of the scalar domain, not the root design.
tasks.evidence: Refactored native support/source construction around typed LalinNative support-domain ASDL. `lua/lalin/native_template_support.lua` now constructs NativeMachineScalarRep values, typed registers, ABI param/result placements, register/scalar/ABI support entries, full host scalar support domains (integer/float/pointer/index) plus the i32 proof-slice domain, and support-domain bank IDs. Added ASDL leaf methods for scalar -> CodeType/token/register class/extension/register placement facts. `lua/lalin/native_template_sources.lua` now derives NativeTemplateBankRequest sources via `NativeTemplateSupportDomain:native_template_bank_request()` / `NativeScalarSupport:append_native_template_sources()` / scalar and Code-axis leaf methods rather than string source-builder slots; the existing i32 proof slice is now a support-domain specialization. Updated `tests/code_ir/test_native_template_sources.lua` to assert the full support domain names i64/f64/pointer reps and that the i32 source set is generated from the ASDL support domain. tasks.title: Author parametric fragment templates for scalar code families
tasks.detail: Author fragment NativeTemplateSource values for scalar Code families across the declared support domain, not only callable i32 helpers. Required fragment categories: constants/immediates for integer widths and pointer/index where representable; integer add/sub/mul/bitwise/shift where semantically compatible; float add/sub/mul/div for f32/f64; unary neg/not/bitnot where compatible; compare fragments producing bool8; alias/register transfer fragments across the finite register locations; return fragments for scalar ABI return classes. Use assembly for inline fragments where C would emit function prologue/ret. Every source declares typed family axes/protocol/operand contracts from ASDL. Missing methods remain absent; do not add placeholder stubs.

tasks.title: Implement ASDL-owned parametric Code graph construction
tasks.detail: Implement CodeFunc/CodeBlock/CodeInstOp/CodeTermOp graph construction using the ASDL placement/ABI model. The builder maps Code params/values to NativeTemplateValueId placements through typed NativeValuePlacement values, selects fragment templates by full NativeTemplateFamily, creates NativeTemplateNode input/output placements, NativeValueEdges, NativeControlEdges, and NativePatchBindings. Start with single-block scalar functions but the code must be parameterized over the support domain's scalar reps/register classes, not hard-coded to i32/eax/ecx. Methods for unimplemented semantic leaves are absent/nil; do not install placeholder errors or return missing/unsupported values.
tasks.evidence: Implemented ASDL-owned scalar Code graph construction in `lua/lalin/native_code_methods.lua` for single-block scalar CodeFunc/CodeBlock with params, CodeInstConst, CodeInstBinary, CodeInstFloatBinary, CodeInstCompare, and CodeTermReturn. The builder uses NativeCodeGraphBuilderState/NativeCodeValuePlacementEntry ASDL state, typed NativeValuePlacement/NativeValueEdge/node inputs/outputs, full NativeTemplateFamily selection, and ABI param/result register transfer templates instead of register side tables. Added schema products for code graph builder state/input, extended support-domain ABI param register modeling to param0/param1, added param transfer NativeTemplateSource fragments, and added NativeCallReturnScalar execution for scalar call args/results. Smoke-compiled and executed generated native CodeFunc values against a generated/imported full scalar bank: i32 add returned 7, i64 add returned 30, f64 add returned 3.75, and i32 less-than compare returned 1. Checks run: `luajit tests/run.lua schema`, `luajit tests/run.lua code_ir` (42 passed, 1 skipped).

tasks.title: Add C continuation/frame stencil ASDL
tasks.detail: Implement the C-only stencil factory protocol documented in docs/RESIDUAL_NATIVE_ARCHITECTURE.md. Add ASDL for C template extraction modes such as entry callable, continuation fragment, and terminal continuation; NativeFrameSlotId, NativeFrameSlot, NativeFrameLayout; frame-size/frame-offset patch coordinates; and typed continuation relocation/edge bindings. NativeTemplateSource/extraction must declare C protocol, expected extern continuation symbols, holes, and target-specific relocation expectations. No handwritten assembly in the solution; no Lua side tables for frame slots.

Updated docs schema sketch. Schema tests pass: luajit tests/run.lua schema.

tasks.title: Implement object verifier for C continuation stencils
tasks.detail: Extend offline generator to compile NativeTemplateC sources with gcc/clang -O3, parse object/ELF sections/symbols/relocations, and verify the declared C protocol. Continuation fragments have signature void(uint8_t *frame), read/write frame slot holes, and end in declared extern continuation calls/tail calls represented by relocations. Entry callables have public ABI signature and a relocation to the first continuation. Terminal continuations return to the entry callable.
